item.title: What to Do in Dumbo If You’re Here for Business (2026)

item.title: The South Carolina Measles Outbreak Is Slowing Down
item.description: The biggest US measles outbreak in decades may be over sooner than expected.
item.media:keywords: vaccines, CDC, health, public health, measles
item.dc:creator: Emily Mullin
item.dc:subject: Public Health

item.title: How the Iran War Could Jack Up Prices on Store Shelves
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz, a busy waterway off the coast of Iran, has come to a near standstill. If the war drags on much longer, it could mean higher prices for consumer goods around the world.
item.media:keywords: Iran, shipping, logistics, supply chain, tariffs, war
item.dc:creator: Aarian Marshall
item.dc:subject: Waterworks

item.title: Grammarly Is Facing a Class Action Lawsuit Over Its AI ‘Expert Review’ Feature
item.description: The feature, which Grammarly shut down Wednesday, presented editing suggestions as if they came from established authors and academics—without their consent.
item.media:keywords: artificial intelligence, Books, college, Creative writing, authors
item.dc:creator: Miles Klee
item.dc:subject: Clone Wars

item.title: Iran Warns US Tech Firms Could Become Targets as War Expands
item.description: Companies including Google, Microsoft, and Palantir were listed as targets by Iranian media as the conflict with Israel and the US spills into digital infrastructure.
item.media:keywords: Google, Microsoft, Iran, Palantir, Middle East, war
item.dc:creator: Dana Alomar
item.dc:subject: Threat Levels

item.media:keywords: Shopping, Deals, Bluetooth, speaker

item.title: This Digital Picture Frame Wants to Bring People Closer to a Holographic Future
item.description: Brooklyn-based Looking Glass has been dabbling in 3D screens for nearly a decade. It's finally launching Musubi, an AI-powered holographic frame to bring your photos and videos to life.

item.title: Meta Ramps Up Efforts to Disrupt Industrialized Scamming
item.description: Meta removed 10.9 million Facebook and Instagram accounts linked to “criminal scam centers” last year, the company announced on Wednesday.

item.title: Teens Are Using AI-Fueled ‘Slander Pages’ to Mock Their Teachers

item.title: Fake AI Content About the Iran War Is All Over X
item.description: X’s Grok is failing to accurately verify video footage from the Iran conflict and is sharing its own AI-generated images about the war.

item.dc:subject: Fake News

item.title: A Trumpworld Events Company Is Raking In Millions in Federal Contracts
item.description: The Trump administration has awarded Event Strategies several contracts—including one that could be worth up to $100 million—with little competition, according to federal filings.

item.dc:subject: Big Business

item.title: GPS Attacks Near Iran Are Wreaking Havoc on Delivery and Mapping Apps
item.description: Delivery apps are glitching and navigation routes are changing abruptly thanks to electronic warfare disrupting the satellite signals that power everything from missiles to your ride home.

item.dc:creator: Carla Sertin

item.title: Apple MacBook Neo Review: Delicious, Low-Hanging Fruit

item.title: Yann LeCun Raises $1 Billion to Build AI That Understands the Physical World
